entity.summary: Canadian centralized cryptocurrency exchange that collapsed in 2019 after customer assets became inaccessible and was later described by Ontario Securities Commission staff as a fraud and Ponzi scheme.
entity.notes: Death date follows the Nova Scotia creditor-protection order and practical exchange collapse. Later OSC findings are used to classify the terminal reason as scam_rug rather than a simple insolvency.

events.title: QuadrigaCX launched
events.description: QuadrigaCX operated as a Canadian cryptocurrency exchange founded in 2013.
events.notes: Year-level launch date based on public company history references.

events.title: Creditor protection granted after assets became inaccessible
events.description: A Nova Scotia court granted Quadriga creditor protection while the exchange sought access to missing or inaccessible customer assets after CEO Gerald Cotten's death.
events.notes: Used as death_date because the exchange was no longer operating normally and was under court protection.

events.title: OSC staff described Quadriga as fraud and Ponzi scheme
events.description: Ontario Securities Commission staff concluded that Quadriga's collapse resulted from fraud by its co-founder and that the platform operated as a Ponzi scheme.
events.notes: Follow-up classification event used to support the scam_rug death_reason.

evidence.title: Quadriga company history reference
evidence.notes: Used only for basic launch-year and entity-background cross-checking; not used as primary collapse evidence.

evidence.title: A Crypto Exchange CEO Dies With the Only Key to $137 Million
evidence.notes: Used for creditor-protection timing, inaccessible customer funds, and operational-collapse context.

evidence.title: The QuadrigaCX crypto mystery deepens as wallets turn up empty
evidence.notes: Used for EY wallet findings and missing customer-funds context.

evidence.title: QuadrigaCX: A Review by Staff of the Ontario Securities Commission
evidence.notes: Primary regulatory source for the fraud and Ponzi-scheme classification.
